record.title: Hershiel A. Tribble, 1973-07-23, Interview #2 - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Parents settled in 1880's; mother was from Willamette Valley, Oregon
record.keywords: police officers literary dating marriage farming moonshine gambling guns immigrants logging camps lumberjacks languages world wars IWW unions strikes railroads gyppos foremen logging

point.title: Pam Malone, Bovill's sheriff
point.synopsis: What Pat Malone was like, as Bovill's sheriff.

point.title: Medicine; Contests for a popular girl; the Great Kamama's healing linament
point.synopsis: Medicine show tricks: contests for a popular girl; scissors that cut glass; the Great Kamama's healing linament; new gadgets for suckers.

point.title: Entertainment; literary debater subjects
point.synopsis: Entertainment, such as house sweats. Literary debate subjects.

point.title: Courtship methods; husband expected to make a living; the woman is to work at home
point.synopsis: Courtship methods. Old forms of entertainment compared with new. A newly married couple sets up house. The husband expected to make a living, the woman to work at home.

point.title: Farmwork done by exchange; owning a stump ranch
point.synopsis: Farmwork done by exchange. Owning a stump ranch.

point.title: Bovill compared to other backwoods towns; violence
point.synopsis: Bovill not much compared to other backwoods towns. Little serious violence.

point.title: High moral standards among loggers
point.synopsis: T. P. Jones insists on high moral standards among the loggers.

point.title: How a moonshiner could be successful; still camouflages as a cedar tree
point.synopsis: Jacks didn't drink on the job. How a moonshiner could be successful, and not get caught. A still camouflaged as a cedar tree.

point.title: Gambler pulls a gun; gambling accepted by management
point.synopsis: A gambler pulls a gun. Gambling is accepted by management.

point.title: Foreigners in logging camps; buying war bonds
point.synopsis: Foreigners in the logging camps. Many speak Slavonian, a "universal" language. They won't buy war bonds, but will gladly give a man money. They disappear after we get into the war. They're friendly, open people.

point.title: Bad conditions in early bunkhouses; getting rid of "greybacks"
point.synopsis: Bad conditions in early bunkhouses. The stench. A way to get rid of "greybacks" (body lice).

point.title: IWW role in improving conditions; 4-L delegate; camp goes on strike
point.synopsis: IWW role in improving conditions. They try to persuade Hershiel to join. Their unreasonable talk at Spokane meeting, which Hershiel attends as a 4-L delegate. The 4-L's want to bring both sides together. Hershiel's camp goes on strike. The strikers disappear. Most IWW's are foreigners.

point.title: Early railroad strike
point.synopsis: An early railroad strike - greenhorns worked.

point.title: Loggers hired from Spokane
point.synopsis: Loggers hired from Spokane. Hershiel has trouble clerking for 30 new men. One logger lives like a king in Spokane.

point.title: Salary costs to Potlatch during strike; men deputized to carry guns
point.synopsis: Salary costs to Potlatch during strike. Men deputized to carry guns; why Hershiel stopped carrying his.

point.title: High regard for managemnet
point.synopsis: Hershiel had high regard for management. T. P. Jones tries to hold Hershiel's job for him.

point.title: Tricks of scaling; log measuring; cheating on scales; methods of scaling defective logs
point.synopsis: Tricks of scaling. Scalers protected by inspections. Variations in log measuring. Hershiel protects woods scalers. He doesn't offend a friend by telling him his log is a cull. He has a complainer scale for himself. T. P. Jones said, "If there's a doubt, give it to the workingman." Cheating on scales leads to more work for scaler. Methods of scaling defective logs. Separate records for various species and lengths.

point.title: Al species were logged; costs of relogging an area; clearcutting vs. leave trees
point.synopsis: All species were logged. Limbing was a chore. Prohibitive costs of relogging an area. Clearcutting vs. leave trees.

record.description: Foreign workers and conditions in Potlatch logging camps. Unreasonable IWW demands in 1917 strike; his role as Four-L representative Tricks of log scaling. Medicine show come-ons. Courtship and marriage. 7-23-73 1.5 hr 50p
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
